columns.description: Lowercase SHA-256 from stable_digest of the exact-key payload {adjudication_series_id, canonical_asset_id, canonical_composite_figi, decision_version, disposition, evidence_digest, identity_case_id, namespace: ame_stocks.identity.adjudication, reason_code, reason_detail, rule_version: s7_identity_adjudication_id_v1, supersedes_identity_adjudication_id}. Nullable values are explicit JSON null; approval fields are excluded to avoid a receipt-subject digest cycle.
columns.name: identity_adjudication_id

columns.description: Lowercase SHA-256 from stable_digest of the exact-key payload {identity_case_id, namespace: ame_stocks.identity.adjudication_series, rule_version: s7_identity_adjudication_series_id_v1}. All revisions of one case share this series ID.
columns.name: adjudication_series_id

columns.description: Positive append-only decision version within adjudication_series_id, starting at 1 and increasing by exactly one.
columns.name: decision_version

columns.description: Immediately prior immutable identity_adjudication_id in the same series; null exactly for version 1. A successor never deletes or rewrites its predecessor.
columns.name: supersedes_identity_adjudication_id

columns.description: Lowercase SHA-256 from stable_digest of the candidate-manifest exact-key payload {detector_rule_version, episode_source_record_set_digest, episode_valid_from_session, episode_valid_through_session, left_outer_composite_figi, left_outer_source_record_id, middle_observed_composite_figi, namespace: ame_stocks.identity.provider_figi_bounce_case, right_outer_composite_figi, right_outer_source_record_id, rule_version: s7_provider_figi_bounce_case_id_v1, six_release_binding_id, ticker}. Exact case payload is verified against the content-addressed candidate manifest.
columns.name: identity_case_id

columns.description: First XNYS session on which the complete A-to-B-to-A case and its content-addressed candidate manifest are available; it includes the right-side A evidence and is never backdated to the B episode.
columns.name: identity_case_available_session

columns.description: Exact case-sensitive S4 provider ticker for this episode; no trimming, casefolding, root parsing, or global ticker mapping is allowed.
columns.name: observed_ticker

columns.description: Exact syntactically valid provider-observed middle-B Composite FIGI; the raw observation remains immutable under every disposition.
columns.name: observed_composite_figi

columns.description: First session in the maximal contiguous middle-B run on the global S4 source-session spine, inclusive.
columns.name: episode_valid_from_session

columns.description: Last session in the maximal contiguous middle-B run on the global S4 source-session spine, inclusive.
columns.name: episode_valid_through_session

columns.description: Positive count of distinct exact S4 selected_source_record_id values in the complete middle-B episode.
columns.name: episode_source_record_count

columns.description: Lowercase SHA-256 of json.dumps(sorted(unique selected_source_record_id values), allow_nan=false, ensure_ascii=true, separators=comma/colon) encoded as UTF-8 with no newline. Override scope matches only the complete set.
columns.name: episode_source_record_set_digest

columns.description: Fixed s7_provider_figi_bounce_detector_v1: exact ticker maximal FIGI runs on the global S4 source-session spine; ticker absence, inactive membership, or global-session gap breaks a run; outer A values equal, middle B differs and is valid, and B lasts at most 20 sessions.
columns.name: detector_rule_version

columns.description: Canonical sorted JSON array of detector reason codes, including 1, 2-5, or 6-20 session band and S5/S6/hierarchy-support flags. These are review evidence, never automatic dispositions.
columns.name: detector_reason_codes

columns.description: Exact approved domain confirmed_genuine_transition, confirmed_provider_contamination, or adjudicated_unresolved. The last is the append-only way to retain or withdraw a case to unresolved without deleting prior decisions.
columns.name: disposition

columns.description: Canonical research Composite FIGI; equals observed for confirmed genuine, differs for confirmed contamination, and is null for adjudicated_unresolved.
columns.name: canonical_composite_figi

columns.description: Deterministic asset ID reproduced from canonical_composite_figi; non-null for the two confirmed dispositions and null for adjudicated_unresolved.
columns.name: canonical_asset_id

columns.description: True exactly for confirmed_provider_contamination; false for confirmed_genuine_transition and adjudicated_unresolved.
columns.name: canonical_override

columns.description: Versioned controlled reason code. Majority, recency, longest run, prior/next value, returns, factor values, and backtest outcomes are forbidden reasons.
columns.name: reason_code

columns.description: Non-empty immutable human rationale included verbatim in identity_adjudication_id, so it cannot change under the same decision ID.
columns.name: reason_detail

columns.description: Canonical sorted JSON evidence manifest with exact dataset, release, source-record, field, session, and availability references used by the decision.
columns.name: evidence_refs

columns.description: Lowercase SHA-256 of the exact canonical evidence_refs bytes.
columns.name: evidence_digest

columns.description: Positive count of distinct structured evidence records in evidence_refs.
columns.name: evidence_record_count

columns.description: Count of evidence_refs backed by an immutable external-source snapshot rather than the six fixed Massive-derived releases. Zero requires null external-manifest fields; a positive value requires one content-addressed manifest.
columns.name: external_evidence_record_count

columns.description: Optional immutable manifest ID for captured external evidence such as an SEC filing, exchange or FINRA notice, issuer announcement, or reviewed identifier reference. The manifest stores source authority class, URL, captured bytes/path/SHA, publication and retrieval timestamps, exact asserted fields, and availability; a live URL alone is invalid.
columns.name: source_external_evidence_manifest_id

columns.description: Lowercase SHA-256 of the exact optional external-evidence manifest bytes; jointly null or non-null with source_external_evidence_manifest_id.
columns.name: source_external_evidence_manifest_sha256

columns.description: Maximum source_available_session among all admitted evidence refs. For external evidence, availability cannot precede either the source publication/filing time or immutable capture time mapped to the bound XNYS calendar.
columns.name: evidence_cutoff_session

columns.description: Immutable row-specific approval receipt ID binding identity_adjudication_id, decision-plan ID/SHA, candidate-manifest ID/SHA, actor, decision, and timestamp.
columns.name: approval_id

columns.description: Normalized relative path to the immutable row-specific approval receipt; no secret, credential, or absolute path is allowed.
columns.name: approval_receipt_path

columns.description: Lowercase SHA-256 of the exact approval receipt bytes.
columns.name: approval_receipt_sha256

columns.description: Fixed effective-registry value approved. Rejection/draft receipts remain control history; withdrawal is represented by an approved adjudicated_unresolved successor row.
columns.name: approval_status

columns.description: Non-empty stable reviewer identity from the approval receipt.
columns.name: approved_by

columns.description: UTC timestamp of the explicit row-specific approval receipt.
columns.name: approved_at_utc

columns.description: First XNYS session whose official open UTC timestamp is strictly after approved_at_utc under the bound calendar.
columns.name: approval_available_session

columns.description: Official XNYS open UTC timestamp for adjudication_available_session under the bound calendar.
columns.name: adjudication_available_at_utc

columns.description: Maximum of identity_case_available_session, evidence_cutoff_session, and approval_available_session. A cutoff-bound resolver cannot use this version earlier.
columns.name: adjudication_available_session

columns.description: Fixed s7_adjudication_availability_from_case_evidence_approval_v1 rule.
columns.name: availability_rule_version

columns.description: Exact versioned XNYS calendar artifact ID used for episode continuity and availability mapping.
columns.name: availability_calendar_id

columns.description: Lowercase SHA-256 of the exact bound XNYS calendar artifact bytes.
columns.name: availability_calendar_sha256

columns.description: Fixed s7_identity_adjudication_v1 exact payload, episode scope, terminal-chain-head, and append-only revision rule.
columns.name: adjudication_rule_version

columns.description: Fixed cutoff_bound_revised_identity_not_factor_signal_v1 marker. A resolver selects a unique terminal chain head from an exact pinned registry release and explicit cutoff; it never mutates a later table by simple masking.
columns.name: adjudication_time_scope

columns.description: Always false. Returns, factor values, portfolio results, and backtest outcomes are prohibited adjudication evidence.
columns.name: outcome_or_backtest_evidence_used

columns.description: Exact protected S4 three-table release-set ID.
columns.name: source_s4_release_set_id

columns.description: Exact S5 ticker-event request-status release ID used only as review evidence.
columns.name: source_s5_status_release_id

columns.description: Exact S5 ticker-change-event release ID used only as review evidence.
columns.name: source_s5_event_release_id

columns.description: Exact S6 ticker-overview-safe release ID used only as retrospective review evidence.
columns.name: source_s6_overview_release_id

columns.description: Immutable candidate manifest ID containing the full detector case payload and bounded evidence.
columns.name: source_identity_case_candidate_manifest_id

columns.description: Lowercase SHA-256 of the exact immutable candidate manifest bytes.
columns.name: source_identity_case_candidate_manifest_sha256

columns.description: Stable ID of the decision-plan logical payload. The logical payload contains adjudication IDs and candidate references but excludes approval fields and its own ID/SHA.
columns.name: source_decision_plan_id

columns.description: Normalized relative path to the immutable decision-plan bytes targeted by the approval receipt.
columns.name: source_decision_plan_path

columns.description: Lowercase SHA-256 of the exact decision-plan bytes, computed before any approval receipt exists.
columns.name: source_decision_plan_sha256

description: Protected append-only registry of approved episode-scoped identity decision versions. The order is immutable candidate manifest, optional captured external-evidence manifest, decision-plan payload and bytes, row-specific approval receipt, then registry row. It preserves provider observations, permits explicit withdrawal to adjudicated_unresolved, and is independently published before the four S7 derived outputs consume one exact release.
grain: One immutable approved decision version for one deterministic provider-FIGI bounce case and exact middle-B episode.

qa_rules.description: Every field, order, Arrow type, and nullability must equal this contract.

The exact six evidence releases, candidate manifest, any referenced external-evidence manifest and captured artifacts, decision plan, approval receipts, and calendar artifact must pass their complete trust chains before registry publication.
Every row and registry build binds one exact six-release bundle, candidate manifest, optional external-evidence manifest, decision plan, receipt, and calendar. Latest-by-time discovery is forbidden.
identity_adjudication_id must be non-null and unique.

Case, series, adjudication, asset, episode-record-set, evidence, candidate-manifest, plan, receipt, and calendar IDs/digests must reproduce exactly from frozen payloads and bytes; fixed vectors must pass.

Each series begins at version 1 with null predecessor; later versions increment by one and reference the immediately prior row. At a cutoff, the unique highest available complete-chain version is terminal; once its successor is available, the predecessor ceases to be effective but remains stored.

The episode must equal the complete maximal middle-B run on the global source-session spine. Dates, ticker, FIGI, positive count, sorted-unique record-set digest, and every source row must match exactly; absence, inactive membership, or a global session gap terminates the run.

confirmed_genuine_transition requires canonical=observed, non-null asset, override=false; confirmed_provider_contamination requires canonical!=observed, non-null asset, override=true; adjudicated_unresolved requires null canonical/asset and override=false. All require approved status and immutable case lineage.

evidence_refs must be canonical sorted JSON; SHA-256, total count, external count, referenced immutable artifacts, and evidence_cutoff_session must reproduce exactly. External availability is no earlier than both publication and capture availability.

external_evidence_record_count=0 requires both external-manifest fields null; a positive count requires a trusted content-addressed manifest whose every record binds source authority class, normalized URL, immutable captured path/bytes/SHA, publication and retrieval timestamps, exact assertion, availability, and evidence_ref. An uncaptured live URL, mutable page, missing timestamp, unavailable artifact, or manifest digest mismatch is invalid. External evidence informs review but never auto-overrides provider identity or bypasses row-specific approval.

Workflow order must be candidate manifest then decision-plan logical payload/bytes then row-specific receipt then registry. Each accepted receipt binds exact plan/candidate/adjudication IDs and SHAs, actor, approved decision, and timestamp; plan bytes contain no approval field or self SHA.
One adjudication ID may bind exactly one accepted approval receipt tuple globally; conflicting actor/time/plan/candidate/receipt bindings fail rather than selecting one.

approval_available_session is the first bound-calendar open strictly after approval; adjudication_available_session is max(case, evidence, approval); adjudication_available_at_utc is its official open. All rule/calendar fields must match.

For each series and explicit resolution cutoff within one pinned registry release, select exactly the highest complete-chain version with availability not after cutoff. No available version means pending unresolved; simple masking of a later materialized row is forbidden.
After terminal-head selection, one exact S4 source row may match at most one effective decision; overlaps fail rather than using recency, version, or input order as a tie-breaker.

A confirmed contamination target must be independently anchored by eligible, non-conflicted direct S4 evidence outside the B episode; S6-only, suspected, or adjudicated rows cannot create the target. Confirmed genuine promotes only its own observed B episode.
No return, factor, portfolio, or backtest outcome may be used as adjudication evidence.

Every observed-to-different-canonical mapping matches one exact full episode and approved decision. Majority, longest run, prior/next/latest value, partial record sets, or global B-to-A rules are forbidden.
The candidate manifest must report all v1 cases by 1, 2-5, and 6-20 session bands, support-reason counts, case availability, and bounded examples before review.
